title: Data structure
text: Start.   writing script follow along, use packages article: Load data tables.   Data tables can loaded individually collectively needed.
code: library(midfielddata) library(data.table) library(wrapr) # Load one table as needed data(student)  # Or load multiple tables data(course, term, degree)

title: Data structure
text: Student-level demographic information 97,555 degree-seeking undergraduate students. Data “student-level” refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students matriculation, example, age, sex, race/ethnicity. student data table 97,555 observations 13 variables listed . View data dictionary using ?student. Student data structured row-record form, , information associated particular ID occupies single row—one record per student. , ID variable primary key, well key common four data sets. can verify column provides unique key follows: Student IDs institution names anonymized remove identifiable information. Race/ethnicity sex often used grouping variables. Counts category.
code: Variable     Class 1            mcid character 2     institution character 3        transfer character 4  hours_transfer   numeric 5            race character 6             sex character 7        age_desc character 8      us_citizen character 9        home_zip character 10    high_school character 11       sat_math   numeric 12     sat_verbal   numeric 13       act_comp   numeric # First few rows and last few rows student #>                  mcid   institution              transfer hours_transfer #>                <char>        <char>                <char>          <num> #>     1: MCID3111142225 Institution B   First-Time Transfer             NA #>     2: MCID3111142283 Institution J   First-Time Transfer             NA #>     3: MCID3111142290 Institution J   First-Time Transfer             NA #>    ---                                                                   #> 97553: MCID3112898894 Institution B First-Time in College             NA #> 97554: MCID3112898895 Institution B First-Time in College             NA #> 97555: MCID3112898940 Institution B First-Time in College             NA #>                 race    sex age_desc us_citizen home_zip high_school sat_math #>               <char> <char>   <char>     <char>   <char>      <char>    <num> #>     1:         Asian   Male Under 25        Yes     <NA>        <NA>       NA #>     2:         Asian Female Under 25        Yes    22020        <NA>      560 #>     3:         Asian   Male Under 25        Yes    23233      471872      510 #>    ---                                                                        #> 97553:         White Female Under 25        Yes    53716      501160      510 #> 97554:         White Female Under 25        Yes    53029      500853      420 #> 97555: Other/Unknown   Male Under 25        Yes    20016      090073      470 #>        sat_verbal act_comp #>             <num>    <num> #>     1:         NA       NA #>     2:        230       NA #>     3:        380       NA #>    ---                     #> 97553:        590       24 #> 97554:        590       32 #> 97555:        540       32 # Check that the indicated column forms a primary key key_col_names <- c("mcid") wrapr::checkColsFormUniqueKeys(student, key_col_names) #> [1] TRUE  # Primary key variable should have no missing values sum(is.na(student$mcid)) #> [1] 0 # Anonymized IDs sample(student$mcid, 8) #> [1] "MCID3111312879" "MCID3112197606" "MCID3111735491" "MCID3112243871" #> [5] "MCID3112849521" "MCID3112669477" "MCID3111754188" "MCID3112290710"  # Anonymized institutions sort(unique(student$institution)) #> [1] "Institution B" "Institution C" "Institution J" # Possible values sort(unique(student$race)) #> [1] "Asian"           "Black"           "Hispanic"        "International"   #> [5] "Native American" "Other/Unknown"   "White"  # Possible values sort(unique(student$sex)) #> [1] "Female"  "Male"    "Unknown" # N by institution student[order(institution), .N, by = "institution"] #>      institution     N #>           <char> <int> #> 1: Institution B 45660 #> 2: Institution C 26712 #> 3: Institution J 25183  # N by race student[order(race), .N, by = "race"] #>               race     N #>             <char> <int> #> 1:           Asian  4193 #> 2:           Black  1860 #> 3:        Hispanic  5386 #> 4:   International  7354 #> 5: Native American   403 #> 6:   Other/Unknown  4509 #> 7:           White 73850  # N by sex student[order(sex), .N, by = "sex"] #>        sex     N #>     <char> <int> #> 1:  Female 46403 #> 2:    Male 51151 #> 3: Unknown     1

title: Data structure
text: Student-level course information 97,555 undergraduates including course name number, credit hours, student grades individual students term course. course data table 3,289,532 observations 12 variables listed . View data dictionary using ?course. Course data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per term per course. , key composite four variables: student ID, course abbreviation number, term. Sample course names. course names contribute composite key, thus NAs possible. Possible values faculty rank variable. Using course abbreviation number keys, can count number unique courses data.
code: Variable     Class 1                 mcid character 2          institution character 3          term_course character 4               course character 5               abbrev character 6               number character 7              section character 8                 type character 9         faculty_rank character 10        hours_course   numeric 11               grade character 12 discipline_midfield character # First few rows and last few rows course #>                    mcid   institution term_course                   course #>                  <char>        <char>      <char>                   <char> #>       1: MCID3111142225 Institution B       19881       Microprocessor Lab #>       2: MCID3111142225 Institution B       19881           Neural Signals #>       3: MCID3111142225 Institution B       19881      Engineering Economy #>      ---                                                                   #> 3289530: MCID3112898940 Institution B       20181     Beginning Japanese 1 #> 3289531: MCID3112898940 Institution B       20181  Precalculus Mathematics #> 3289532: MCID3112898940 Institution B       20181 Deviance In U S  Society #>          abbrev number section         type faculty_rank hours_course  grade #>          <char> <char>  <char>       <char>       <char>        <num> <char> #>       1:   ECEN   2230     005         <NA>         <NA>            1      C #>       2:   ECEN   4811     001         <NA>         <NA>            3      C #>       3:   MCEN   4147     001         <NA>         <NA>            3     B+ #>      ---                                                                     #> 3289530:   JPNS   1010     009 Face-to-Face     Lecturer            5      C #> 3289531:   MATH   1150     012 Face-to-Face     Lecturer            4     C- #> 3289532:   SOCY   1004     100 Face-to-Face   Instructor            3      B #>                           discipline_midfield #>                                        <char> #>       1: Engineering: Electrical and Computer #>       2: Engineering: Electrical and Computer #>       3:              Engineering: Mechanical #>      ---                                      #> 3289530:    Language and Literature: Japanese #> 3289531:                          Mathematics #> 3289532:           Social Sciences: Sociology # Check that the indicated column forms a primary key key_col_names <- c("mcid", "term_course", "abbrev", "number") wrapr::checkColsFormUniqueKeys(course, key_col_names) #> [1] TRUE   # Composite key variables should have no missing values sum(is.na(course$mcid)) #> [1] 0 sum(is.na(course$term_course)) #> [1] 0 sum(is.na(course$abbrev)) #> [1] 0 sum(is.na(course$number)) #> [1] 0 # Sample course names sample(course$course, 10) #>  [1] "Sociology Of Aging"                      #>  [2] NA                                        #>  [3] "War/Peace/Strat Defense"                 #>  [4] "Campaigns And Revolutions"               #>  [5] NA                                        #>  [6] "College Composition"                     #>  [7] "Ethics"                                  #>  [8] NA                                        #>  [9] "Shakespeare/Nonmajors"                   #> [10] "Global Issues And International Affairs" # Possible values sort(unique(course$faculty_rank)) #>  [1] "Academic Professional"     "Adjunct Faculty"           #>  [3] "Administrative Assistant"  "Assistant Professor"       #>  [5] "Associate Professor"       "Clinical Faculty"          #>  [7] "Graduate Assistant"        "Instructor"                #>  [9] "Lecturer"                  "Non-Academic Professional" #> [11] "Other"                     "Pre/Post Doctoral"         #> [13] "Professor"                 "Research Faculty"          #> [15] "Senior Administrator"      "Technician/Specialist"     #> [17] "Undergraduate Assistant"   "Visiting Faculty" # Extract unique courses DT <- copy(course) DT <- unique(DT[, .(abbrev, number)]) DT #>        abbrev number #>        <char> <char> #>     1:   ECEN   2230 #>     2:   ECEN   4811 #>     3:   MCEN   4147 #>    ---               #> 22985:   ENVD   1976 #> 22986:   ORGN   1001 #> 22987:   IPHY   2010  # Number of unique courses nrow(DT) #> [1] 22987

title: Data structure
text: Student-level term information 135 undergraduates including program code, academic standing, grade point average individual students term. term data table 639,915 observations 13 variables listed . View data dictionary using ?term. Term data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per term. , key composite two variables: student ID term. Terms encoded YYYYT, YYYY year start academic year, T encodes semester quarter—Fall (1), Winter (2), Spring (3), Summer (4, 5, 6)—within academic year example, academic year 1995–96, 19951 encodes Fall 95–96 19953 encodes Spring 95–96 19954 encodes Summer 95–96 (first session) Different institutions supply data different time spans. Programs encoded cip6 variable, 6-digit character based 2010 Classification Instructional Programs (CIP) (NCES 2010). Student level used determining timely completion terms transfer students.
code: Variable     Class 1                 mcid character 2          institution character 3                 term character 4                 cip6 character 5                level character 6             standing character 7                 coop character 8           hours_term   numeric 9   hours_term_attempt   numeric 10         hours_cumul   numeric 11 hours_cumul_attempt   numeric 12            gpa_term   numeric 13           gpa_cumul   numeric # First few rows and last few rows term #>                   mcid   institution   term   cip6         level #>                 <char>        <char> <char> <char>        <char> #>      1: MCID3111142225 Institution B  19881 140901 01 First-year #>      2: MCID3111142283 Institution J  19881 240102 01 First-year #>      3: MCID3111142283 Institution J  19883 240102 01 First-year #>     ---                                                          #> 639913: MCID3112898894 Institution B  20181 451001 01 First-year #> 639914: MCID3112898895 Institution B  20181 302001 01 First-year #> 639915: MCID3112898940 Institution B  20181 050103 01 First-year #>                   standing   coop hours_term hours_term_attempt hours_cumul #>                     <char> <char>      <num>              <num>       <num> #>      1:      Good Standing     No          7                  7           7 #>      2: Academic Probation     No          6                  6           6 #>      3: Academic Probation     No         12                 12          18 #>     ---                                                                     #> 639913:      Good Standing     No         13                 13          13 #> 639914:      Good Standing     No         18                 18          18 #> 639915:      Good Standing     No         15                 15          15 #>         hours_cumul_attempt gpa_term gpa_cumul #>                       <num>    <num>     <num> #>      1:                   7     2.56      2.56 #>      2:                   6     1.85      1.85 #>      3:                  18     1.93      1.90 #>     ---                                        #> 639913:                  13     3.52      3.52 #> 639914:                  18     3.50      3.50 #> 639915:                  15     2.18      2.18 # Check that the indicated column forms a primary key key_col_names <- c("mcid", "term") wrapr::checkColsFormUniqueKeys(term, key_col_names) #> [1] TRUE  # Composite key variables should have no missing values sum(is.na(term$mcid)) #> [1] 0 sum(is.na(term$term)) #> [1] 0 # Range of data by institution term[, .(min_term = min(term), max_term = max(term)), by = "institution"] #>      institution min_term max_term #>           <char>   <char>   <char> #> 1: Institution B    19881    20181 #> 2: Institution J    19881    20096 #> 3: Institution C    19901    20154 # A sample of cip6 values sort(unique(sample(term$cip6, 8))) #> [1] "030103" "140201" "141201" "240199" "260901" "450701" "540101" # Possible values sort(unique(term$level)) #> [1] "01 First-year"      "02 Second-year"     "03 Third-year"      #> [4] "04 Fourth-year"     "05 Fifth-year Plus"

title: Data structure
text: Student-level degree information 49,543 undergraduates including institution, program, term, baccalaureate degree student. degree data table 49,665 observations 5 variables listed . View data dictionary using ?degree. Degree data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per degree. Multiple degrees can occur term different terms. , key composite two variables: student ID CIP code. dataset contains records graduates , thus degree fewer rows student. term_degree variable indicates student graduated cip6 indicates program. Sample degree names. number unique degrees data. Number degrees earned per student. practice data, 122 students earned two degrees.
code: Variable     Class 1        mcid character 2 institution character 3 term_degree character 4        cip6 character 5      degree character # First few rows and last few rows degree #>                  mcid   institution term_degree   cip6 #>                <char>        <char>      <char> <char> #>     1: MCID3111142225 Institution B       19881 141001 #>     2: MCID3111142290 Institution J       19921 141001 #>     3: MCID3111142294 Institution J       19903 141001 #>    ---                                                 #> 49663: MCID3112839623 Institution B       20181 160102 #> 49664: MCID3112845220 Institution B       20181 270101 #> 49665: MCID3112845673 Institution B       20174 090101 #>                                                          degree #>                                                          <char> #>     1:            Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering #>     2:            Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering #>     3:            Bachelor of Science in Electrical Engineering #>    ---                                                          #> 49663:                       Bachelor of Science in Linguistics #> 49664:                       Bachelor of Science in Mathematics #> 49665: Bachelor of Science in Speech Communication and Rhetoric # Check that the indicated column forms a primary key key_col_names <- c("mcid", "cip6") wrapr::checkColsFormUniqueKeys(degree, key_col_names) #> [1] TRUE  # Composite key variables should have no missing values sum(is.na(degree$mcid)) #> [1] 0 sum(is.na(degree$degree)) #> [1] 0 sample(degree$degree, 10) #>  [1] "Bachelor of Science in Aerospace Engineering"                  #>  [2] "Bachelor of Science in Business Administration and Management" #>  [3] "Bachelor of Fine Arts in Theatre Arts"                         #>  [4] "Bachelor of International and Global Studies"                  #>  [5] "Bachelor of Science in Molecular Biology"                      #>  [6] "Bachelor of Arts in Political Science and Government"          #>  [7] "Bachelor of Liberal Arts and Sciences"                         #>  [8] "Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering"                 #>  [9] "Bachelor of Musical Arts"                                      #> [10] "Bachelor of Arts in Geography" # Count the unique degrees length(unique(degree$degree)) #> [1] 153  # Count the number of unique program codes length(unique(degree$cip6)) #> [1] 153 # Count number of degrees by student by_id <- degree[, .(degree_count = .N), by = "mcid"]  # Count the number of students by the degree count by_id[, .(N_students = .N), by = "degree_count"] #>    degree_count N_students #>           <int>      <int> #> 1:            1      49421 #> 2:            2        122

title: Data linked by student ID
text: Start.   writing script follow along, use packages article: Load data tables.   Data tables can loaded individually collectively needed. View data dictionaries help pages using ?student, ?course, ?term, ?degree.
code: library(midfielddata) library(data.table)  # data.table printout option options(datatable.print.nrows = 70) # Load one table as needed data(student)  # Or load multiple tables data(course, term, degree)

what: Student 1: Degree in psychology and sociology
title: Data linked by student ID
text: display records one specific student, using ID subset data table. student.   Yields one row per student. course.   student, course records span 47 rows, one row per course. omit several columns rename others, obtain readable output. Term.   , records span 10 rows, one row per term. , can editing improve readability, assuming variables might need analysis. Degree.   example, records span 2 rows, one row per degree. degrees earned term, Spring 2009.

title: Data linked by student ID
text: Degree.   example, student earned two degrees separate times. Usually interested student records time first degree, ignoring subsequent degrees (unless degrees relevant one’s study). Thus, extracting course term information, filter terms later first degree term—case, term 19961. student.   expected, yields one row. course.   student, records show 52 courses (one row per course) leading thier first degree. Term.   , records show 12 terms (one row per term) leading first degree.
code: # One student  id_we_want <- "MCID3111315508" # Observations for a selected ID, showing selected columns DT <- degree[mcid == id_we_want, .(term_degree, cip6, degree)]  # display DT #>    term_degree   cip6                                     degree #>         <char> <char>                                     <char> #> 1:       19961 260101 Bachelor of Science in Biological Sciences #> 2:       19994 260701      Bachelor of Science in Animal Biology # extract the term of the first degree first_degree_term <- degree[mcid == id_we_want, min(term_degree)]  # display first_degree_term #> [1] "19961" # Observations for a selected ID DT <- student[mcid == id_we_want]  # display DT #>              mcid   institution              transfer hours_transfer #>            <char>        <char>                <char>          <num> #> 1: MCID3111315508 Institution C First-Time in College             NA #>             race    sex age_desc us_citizen home_zip high_school sat_math #>           <char> <char>   <char>     <char>   <char>      <char>    <num> #> 1: Other/Unknown   Male Under 25        Yes    80521        <NA>      610 #>    sat_verbal act_comp #>         <num>    <num> #> 1:        490       NA # columns to keep cols_we_want <- c("term_course", "course", "abbrev", "number", "hours_course", "grade")  # subset DT <- course[mcid == id_we_want, ..cols_we_want]  # retain terms leading up to the first degree DT <- DT[term_course <= first_degree_term]  # order rows DT <- DT[order(term_course, number)]  # merge course abbreviation and number, shorten some column names DT <- DT[, .(term = term_course, course, number = paste0(abbrev, "-", number), cr_hr = hours_course, grade)]  # display DT #>       term                         course number cr_hr  grade #>     <char> title: Authors and Citation
text: Richard Layton. Maintainer, author, copyright holder. Russell Long. Author, copyright holder. Matthew Ohland. Author, copyright holder. Marisa Orr. Author, copyright holder. Susan Lord. Author, copyright holder. US National Science Foundation. Funder.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Layton R, Long R, Ohland M, Orr M, Lord S (2026). midfielddata: MIDFIELD Data Sample. R package version 0.2.3, https://midfieldr.github.io/midfielddata/.
code: @Manual{,   title = {midfielddata: MIDFIELD Data Sample},   author = {Richard Layton and Russell Long and Matthew Ohland and Marisa Orr and Susan Lord},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.2.3},   url = {https://midfieldr.github.io/midfielddata/}, }

title: MIDFIELD Data Sample
text: midfielddata R data package provides anonymized student-level records 98,000 undergraduates three US institutions 1988 2018, collected four data tables keyed student ID: student   students, demographics, transfer status, institution, etc. course   students, every course, course name, number, credit hours, grades, etc. term   students, every term, program, standing, GPA, etc. degree   graduating students, degree(s), program(s), term data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database (Ohland Long 2016), suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata practice, research. Suggested packages. midfieldr working MIDFIELD data (Layton et al. 2026) data.table manipulating data (Barrett et al. 2026)

title: MIDFIELD Data Sample
text: installed size midfielddata 24 Mb, installation take longer conventional CRAN package. Install MIDFIELDR drat repository :
code: install.packages("midfielddata",   repos = "https://MIDFIELDR.github.io/drat/",   type = "source" )

title: MIDFIELD Data Sample
text: Data tables can loaded individually collectively needed. Tutorials: examine data greater detail package articles. one uses data developed detail midfieldr website.
code: library(midfielddata)  # Load one table as needed data(student)  # Or load multiple tables data(course, term, degree)

title: Student-level course data — course
text: Student-level course information approximately 98,000 undergraduates, keyed student ID. Data "student-level" refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students, example, course name number, credit hours, student grades.

title: Student-level course data — course
code: data(course)

title: Student-level course data — course
text: data.frame data.table 12 variables approximately 3.3M observations 97,555 unique students occupying 325 MB memory: mcid Character, de-identified student ID. Key column. institution Character, de-identified institution name, e.g., Institution , Institution B, etc. term Character, academic year term, format YYYYT. Key column. course Character, course name, e.g., Astrophysics  III, Calculus Social Science Business, Corp  Financial Rprtng 1, Environmental Sanitation II, Fitness  Wellness, Introductory Astronomy 2, Changing  Environment, etc. abbrev Character, course alphabetical identifier, e.g. ENGR, MATH, ENGL. Key column. number Character, course numeric identifier, e.g. 101, 3429. Key column. section Character, course section identifier, one four characters, e.g., 1, 2, 01, 14, 001, 040, 785, H02, R01, 300E, 888R, etc. type Character, predominant delivery method section, e.g., Blended, Distance Education, Face--Face, Online, etc. faculty_rank Character, academic rank person teaching course, e.g., Assistant Professor, Associate Professor, Graduate Assistant, Visiting Faculty, etc. hours_course Numeric, number credit-hours successful course completion. grade Character, course grade, e.g., +, , -, B+, , NG, etc. discipline_midfield Character, variable grouping courses academic discipline assigned pre-2023 MIDFIELD data curator, e.g., Anthropology, Business, Computer Science, Engineering, Language Literature, Mathematics,Visual Performing Arts, etc.

title: Student-level course data — course
text: 2022 MIDFIELD database

title: Student-level course data — course
text: Course data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per course per term. Terms encoded YYYYT, YYYY year start academic year T encodes semester quarter within academic year Fall (1), Winter (2), Spring (3), Summer (4, 5, 6). example, academic year 1995–96, Fall 95–96 encoded 19951, Spring 95–96 encoded 19953, first Summer 95-96 term encoded 19954. source database includes special month-long sessions encoded letters , B, C, etc., though none included sample. possible values grade variable includes NG "grade given", W indicating withdrawal. example, lab course may mandatory co-requisite lecture course, grade given lecture course lab grade recorded NG. data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata provides practice data, research data.

title: Student-level course data — course
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Load data data(course)  # Select specific rows and columns rows_we_want <- course$mcid == MCID3112192438 cols_we_want <- c(mcid, term_course, course, grade)  # View observations for this ID course[rows_we_want, cols_we_want] } # }

title: Student-level degree data — degree
text: Student-level degree information approximately 50,000 undergraduates earning degrees, keyed student ID. Data "student-level" refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students, example, institution, program, term, baccalaureate degree.

title: Student-level degree data — degree
code: data(degree)

title: Student-level degree data — degree
text: data.frame data.table 5 variables 49,543 observations unique students occupying 6 MB memory: mcid Character, de-identified student ID. Key column. institution Character, de-identified institution name, e.g., Institution , Institution B, etc. term_degree Character, academic year term student completes program, format YYYYT. cip6 Character, 6-digit CIP code program student enrolled term. Key column. degree Character, type degree awarded, e.g., Bachelor Arts Geography, Bachelor Science Finance, etc.

title: Student-level degree data — degree
text: 2022 MIDFIELD database

title: Student-level degree data — degree
text: Degree data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per degree. Multiple degrees can occur term different terms. Terms encoded YYYYT, YYYY year start academic year T encodes semester quarter within academic year Fall (1), Winter (2), Spring (3), Summer (4, 5, 6). example, academic year 1995–96, Fall 95–96 encoded 19951, Spring 95–96 encoded 19953, first Summer 95-96 term encoded 19954. source database includes special month-long sessions encoded letters , B, C, etc., though none included sample. program codes, midfielddata uses 2010 version Classification Instructional Programs (CIP). midfieldr package installed loaded, type ?cip details. data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata provides practice data, research data.

title: Student-level degree data — degree
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Load data data(degree)  # Select specific rows and columns rows_we_want <- degree$mcid == MCID3112192438 cols_we_want <- c(mcid, term_degree, cip6, degree)  # View observations for this ID degree[rows_we_want, cols_we_want] } # }

title: midfielddata: MIDFIELD Data Sample — midfielddata-package
text: data package contains anonymized student-level records 98,000 undergraduates three US institutions 1988 2018 organized four data tables keyed student ID. Student-level data refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students including demographics, programs academic standing term, individual courses, grades, degrees. data proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—'midfielddata' provides practice data, research data. 'midfieldr' package, available < https://CRAN.R-project.org/package=midfieldr>, designed interact 'midfielddata' well full MIDFIELD database, management transferred American Society Engineering Education 2023. development 'midfieldr' 'midfielddata' supported US National Science Foundation grant numbers 1545667 2142087.

title: midfielddata: MIDFIELD Data Sample — midfielddata-package
text: Maintainer: Richard Layton graphdoctor@gmail.com [copyright holder] Authors: Richard Layton graphdoctor@gmail.com [copyright holder] Russell Long [copyright holder] Matthew Ohland ohland@purdue.edu [copyright holder] Marisa Orr [copyright holder] Susan Lord [copyright holder] contributors: US National Science Foundation [funder]

title: Student-level demographic data — student
text: Student-level demographic information approximately 98,000 degree-seeking undergraduate students, keyed student ID. Data "student-level" refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students, example, age, sex, race/ethnicity matriculation.

title: Student-level demographic data — student
code: data(student)

title: Student-level demographic data — student
text: data.frame data.table 13 variables 97,555 observations unique students occupying 18 MB memory: mcid Character, de-identified student ID. Key column. institution Character, de-identified institution name, e.g., Institution , Institution B, etc. transfer Character, transfer status, possible values First-Time College, First-Time Transfer. hours_transfer Numeric, number credit hours transferred (NA). race Character, race/ethnicity self-reported student, e.g., Asian, Black, Hispanic, etc. sex Character, sex self-reported student, possible values Female, Male, Unknown. age_desc Character, age group, possible values 25 Older, 25. us_citizen Character, US citizenship, possible values , Yes. home_zip Character, home ZIP code (NA), e.g., 02056, 20170, 51301, 80129, etc. high_school Character, code last high school attended admission (NA), e.g., 060075, 210512, 431800, 502195, etc. sat_math Numeric, SAT mathematics test score (NA). sat_verbal Numeric, SAT reading test score (NA). act_comp Numeric, ACT composite test score (NA).

title: Student-level demographic data — student
text: 2022 MIDFIELD database

title: Student-level demographic data — student
text: Student data structured row-record form, , information associated particular ID occupies single row—one record per student. data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata provides practice data, research data.

title: Student-level demographic data — student
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{  # Load data data(student)  # Select specific rows and columns rows_we_want <- student$mcid == MCID3112192438 cols_we_want <- c(mcid, institution, transfer, race, sex, age_desc)  # View observations for this ID student[rows_we_want, cols_we_want] } # }

title: Student-level term data — term
text: Student-level term information approximately 98,000 undergraduates, keyed student ID. Data "student-level" refers information collected undergraduate institutions individual students, example, program code, academic standing, grade point average term.

title: Student-level term data — term
code: data(term)

title: Student-level term data — term
text: data.frame data.table 13 variables approximately 640,000 observations 97,555 unique students occupying 73 MB memory: mcid Character, de-identified student ID. Key column. institution Character, de-identified institution name, e.g., Institution , Institution B, etc. term Character, academic year term, format YYYYT. Key column. cip6 Character, 6-digit CIP code program student enrolled term, e.g., 090101, 141201, 260901, 420101, etc. level Character, 01 Freshman, 02 Sophomore, etc. equivalent values current practice data 01 First-Year, 02-Second Year, etc. standing Character, academic standing, e.g., Good Standing, Academic Warning, etc. coop Character, cooperative education term, possible values Yes, . hours_term Numeric, credit hours earned term. hours_term_attempt Numeric, credit hours attempted term. hours_cumul Numeric, cumulative credit hours earned. hours_cumul_attempt Numeric, cumulative credit hours attempted. gpa_term Numeric, term grade point average. gpa_cumul Numeric, cumulative grade point average.

title: Student-level term data — term
text: 2022 MIDFIELD database

title: Student-level term data — term
text: Term data structured block-record form, , records associated particular ID can span multiple rows—one record per student per term. Terms encoded YYYYT, YYYY year start academic year T encodes semester quarter within academic year Fall (1), Winter (2), Spring (3), Summer (4, 5, 6). example, academic year 1995–96, Fall 95–96 encoded 19951, Spring 95–96 encoded 19953, first Summer 95-96 term encoded 19954. source database includes special month-long sessions encoded letters , B, C, etc., though none included sample. program codes, midfielddata uses 2010 version Classification Instructional Programs (CIP). midfieldr package installed loaded, type ?cip details. data midfielddata proportionate stratified sample MIDFIELD database, suitable drawing inferences program attributes student experiences—midfielddata provides practice data, research data.

title: Student-level term data — term
